rulings.Case Title: CORNELIUS MURPHY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al

rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 6, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Specially appearing Century Indemnity Company's motion for an order setting aside default and default judgment entered against James A. Nelson, Co., Inc. is continued on the court's own motion to April 12, 2017. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD SHORTALL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Setting Aside Default And Default Judgment Entered Against James A Nelson Co Inc
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 6, 2017 in Department 503, Line 2. Specially appearing Century Indemnity Company's motion for order setting aside default and default judgment entered against James A. Nelson Co., Inc. is granted. The June 7, 2012 default and the October 16, 2014 default judgment entered against James A. Nelson Co., Inc. are set aside. There is no opposition to the motion. Plaintiff is not precluded from seeking available relief. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: THOMPSON VS A P GREEN INDUSTRIES INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Syd Carpenter Marine Contractor, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Pursuant To C.C.P 583.310
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 6, 2017 in Department 503, Line 4. Defendant Syd Carpenter Marine Contractor, Inc.'s motion to dismiss is granted pursuant to C.C.P. §§ 583.310 and 583.360. The action is dismissed as to defendant Syd Carpenter Marine Contractor, Inc. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
